nodes.label: AI as thinking partner for brainstorming and feedback
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or unnecessary for creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I thought it would be good to ask AI for help with some of these situations because sometimes, it can be hard to reflect on your own work

nodes.label: AI for clarity and conciseness in writing
nodes.label_negative: AI undermining personal voice or scientific tone
nodes.grounding_span: I occasionally used AI tools to help with clarity/conciseness in my phrasing and proof-reading

nodes.label: AI for literature review and source access
nodes.label_negative: AI limited by paywalls and inability to access scientific databases
nodes.grounding_span: AI wouldn't have access to a lot of the scientific research I was using because the databases are behind paywalls

nodes.label: AI as guidance tool versus information source
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable information source requiring verification
nodes.grounding_span: I didn't use AI for finding specific information or making claims/statements I didn't already have backed up with scientific sources

nodes.label: Research efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: it would save so much time digging through articles

nodes.label: Scientific integrity and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I would always fact-check, just in case

nodes.label: Personal understanding and ownership of research
nodes.grounding_span: I knew enough about the topic and the plans I had for my research to know whether or not I wanted to incorporate what AI provided

nodes.label: Positive view of AI as thinking partner
nodes.grounding_span: it can be incorporated as a great thinking partner to make the entire process go a bit more smoothly

nodes.label: Cautious optimism about AI for literature review
nodes.grounding_span: I think the literature review process could be an amazing way to use AI and I hope, in the future, that it could be possible

nodes.label: Resistance to over-reliance on AI
nodes.grounding_span: it is important for scientists to not rely on AI to do all their work for them

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through external feedback
nodes.grounding_span: Being able to put all my thoughts, ideas, and background details into an AI model...and being able to say, 'How can I make this aspect of my project more clear?'

nodes.label: Pragmatic boundary-setting between AI assistance and personal expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I knew enough about the topic and the plans I had for my research to know whether or not I wanted to incorporate what AI provided
